key_insight: TypiCore improves class-incremental learning on time series by alternating typicality- and diversity-based queries to build better memory buffers under limited label budgets.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for industrial and biomedical monitoring systems where labeled streams are expensive but models must adapt continuously to new operating conditions.
method: Systematically compares multiple query strategies with rehearsal-based continual learning methods for multivariate time series.
Proposes TypiCore, a hybrid active query policy that switches between typicality and diversity across active-learning cycles.
Evaluates plasticity, stability, and label efficiency on the TSCIL benchmark across four datasets.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports statistically significant improvements over all baselines and performance that matches or surpasses fully supervised continual learning on multiple datasets using only a fraction of available labels.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful buffer management and the chosen active-learning cycle schedule, so gains may not transfer unchanged to all streaming settings.
paper_title: TypiCore: A Hybrid Active Query Strategy for Class-Incremental Learning on Time Series
